frozen_components: Language models (Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 2 2B, Llama 3.2 1B) after pruning
architecture.summary: The paper uses Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) as interpretability probes to analyze how weight pruning reshapes the feature geometry of language models. SAEs are applied to three model families (Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 2 2B, Llama 3.2 1B) after subjecting them to unstructured pruning via magnitude-based or Wanda methods across sparsity levels from 0–60%. The SAE encodes model activations into sparse feature representations; the paper measures survival, transferability, and causal relevance of these features pre- and post-pruning.
trained_components: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) trained to encode language model activations

biblio.title: How Pruning Reshapes Features: Sparse Autoencoder Analysis of Weight-Pruned Language Models

claims.dataset: Three model families (Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 2 2B, Llama 3.2 1B) across two pruning methods and six sparsity levels
claims.statement: Rare SAE features--those with low firing rates--survive pruning far better than frequent ones, with within-condition Spearman correlations of rho = -1.0 in 11 of 17 experimental conditions.
Pruning acts as implicit feature selection, preferentially destroying high-frequency generic features while preserving specialized rare ones.

claims.dataset: Three model families across six sparsity levels
claims.statement: Wanda pruning preserves feature structure up to 3.7x better than magnitude pruning.
Pre-trained SAEs remain viable on Wanda-pruned models up to 50% sparsity.
Geometric feature survival does not predict causal importance--a dissociation with implications for interpretability under compression.
The paper presents the first systematic study of how unstructured pruning reshapes the feature geometry of language models using Sparse Autoencoders as interpretability probes.
